items.title: feat: add /java shared Java processing infrastructure
items.body:
Establishes a root-level `/java/` directory as the single source of truth for Java-based processing across all repo directories — mirroring the `/pwa/` shared-infrastructure pattern.

## Structure

- **`src/main/java/ai/majix/JavaCore.java`** — Core processor: retry-capable pipeline, fluent `Config.Builder`, pluggable `Processor` interface, and `main()` CLI entry-point
- **`pom.xml`** — Parent Maven POM (Java 17, JUnit 5, Shade fat-JAR); sub-projects inherit via `<parent>`
- **`java-runner.js`** — Node.js CLI; auto-builds the JAR when missing, then invokes it from any working directory
- **`template/pom.xml`** — Drop-in POM for new sub-projects; set `<artifactId>` and adjust `<relativePath>`
- **`template/ExampleProcessor.java`** — Reference `Processor` implementation

## Usage

```bash
# Run with defaults (builds JAR on first use)
node java/java-runner.js

# Target a specific sub-project
node java/java-runner.js my-app ./my-app/data results.json

# Force Maven rebuild
node java/java-runner.js --build my-app ./my-app/data results.json
```

Custom processing logic plugs in via `processorClass`:

```java
JavaCore.Config cfg = new JavaCore.Config.Builder()
    .appId("my-app")
    .dataDir("./my-app/data")
    .processorClass("ai.majix.myapp.MyProcessor")
    .maxRetries(3)
    .build();
new JavaCore(cfg).run();
```

New sub-directories wire in by copying `java/template/pom.xml`, setting the artifact ID, and implementing `JavaCore.Processor`.

items.title: Restructure repo: move Python to python/, add Java 21 port in java/
items.body:
Splits the single-file Python tool into a proper `python/` subdirectory and adds a parallel Java 21 implementation in its own `java/` directory, each with full build tooling and cross-platform scripts.

## Python (`python/`)
- Moved `xmlcompare.py`, `tests/`, `samples/`, `requirements.txt` from repo root into `python/`
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — creates venv, installs deps, runs pytest
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — activates venv and delegates to `xmlcompare.py`

## Java (`java/`)
- Full port of all comparison logic in `com.xmlcompare` package: `CompareOptions`, `Difference`, `XmlCompare`, `Main`
- DOM-based XML parsing (`javax.xml`), picocli for CLI, Jackson for JSON/YAML config
- Identical feature set: numeric tolerance, namespace stripping, ordered/unordered children, skip-keys/pattern, XPath filter, text/JSON/HTML output, `--config` file, exit codes 0/1/2
- Dual build: Gradle (`build.gradle` + wrapper) and Maven (`pom.xml` with shade plugin for fat JAR)
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — runs both Gradle and Maven builds
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — invokes the fat JAR via `java -jar`
- 55 JUnit 5 tests covering all major code paths

## Docs & housekeeping
- Root `README.md` rewritten to describe the two-language layout with quick-start commands for each
- `.gitignore` updated: Python `.venv/`, Java `build/`/`target/` per-project rather than repo-global `build/`

```bash
# Python
cd python && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json

# Java
cd java && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json
```

items.title: Fix Java start command JAVA_OPTS variable expansion
items.body:
## Problem

The cafe-webapp deployment was failing at the health check stage with "Could not find or load main class $JAVA_OPTS". Railway's TOML config does not expand shell variables in startCommand, so `$JAVA_OPTS` was passed as a literal string and Java interpreted it as the main class name instead of a JVM flag.

## Solution

Removed `$JAVA_OPTS` from the startCommand in `backend-spring/railway.toml`, leaving `java -jar app.jar`. Since `$JAVA_OPTS` is not defined in the environment, the application does not need it to start correctly, and removing it allows Java to launch as expected and pass the `/health` check.

### Changes
- **Modified** `backend-spring/railway.toml`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

items.title: MergeSort.java
items.body:
Esse código implementa o algoritmo Merge Sort para ordenar um vetor de números inteiros em ordem crescente. Ele funciona dividindo o vetor recursivamente em partes menores até chegar a subvetores de tamanho 1, e depois vai juntando (mesclando) essas partes de forma ordenada.

A função `dividir` separa o vetor em duas metades repetidamente, e a função `mesclar` é responsável por comparar os elementos dessas metades e reorganizá-los no vetor original já em ordem. No final, o vetor inteiro fica ordenado de forma eficiente, com complexidade de tempo O(n log n).

items.title: feat(java): remove guava dependency from fory java
items.body:
## Why?

Make `fory-core` usable without requiring Guava on the runtime classpath while keeping Guava
collection support available when the dependency is present.

## What does this PR do?

- Marks the `guava` dependency in `java/fory-core` as optional.
- Replaces the remaining Guava-based core utilities with local `org.apache.fory.collection`
  implementations for the cache, map maker, bi-map, weak-reference cleanup, and related helpers.
- Keeps Guava collection serializers available behind runtime detection and preserves reserved type IDs
  when Guava registration is disabled or Guava is absent.
- Moves Guava collection serializer coverage into `java/fory-testsuite`, adds explicit test-scope Guava
  dependencies there and in `java/fory-format`, and adds `GuavaOptionalDependencyTest` for the
  no-Guava classpath case.
- Removes a few incidental Guava usages from Java benchmarks/tests and updates native-image
  initialization metadata for the new collection classes.

## Related issues
Closes #1113 
#1114 #1335

## AI Contribution Checklist



- [ ] Substantial AI assistance was used in this PR: `yes` / `no`
- [ ] If `yes`, I included a completed [AI Contribution Checklist](https://github.com/apache/fory/blob/main/AI_POLICY.md#9-contributor-checklist-for-ai-assisted-prs) in this PR description and the required `AI Usage Disclosure`.
- [ ] If `yes`, my PR description includes the required `ai_review` summary and screenshot evidence of the final clean AI review results from both fresh reviewers on the current PR diff or current HEAD after the latest code changes.



## Does this PR introduce any user-facing change?

- [x] `fory-core` no longer requires Guava on the runtime classpath; Guava-specific serializers remain available when Guava is present.

- [x] Does this PR introduce any public API change?
- [ ] Does this PR introduce any binary protocol compatibility change?

## Benchmark

Not run.

items.title: Main.java
items.body:
apresenta um menu para o usuário escolher entre executar testes automáticos ou fazer uma busca manual, e no caso dos testes, trabalha com conjuntos de dados de tamanhos variados, gerando valores aleatórios para simular um cenário real de uso.

Quando os experimentos são executados, o programa aplica três tipos de busca: sequencial, binária e em árvore, várias vezes sobre os mesmos dados, medindo o tempo de execução de cada uma. No final, ele calcula e exibe a média e o desvio padrão desses tempos, permitindo analisar qual algoritmo é mais eficiente em cada situação.
